threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered when the bus driver shouts 'next stop' instead of the usual pause for polite announcements.
Saw a bloke on the 36 singing love songs to an empty seat. Twice, he ordered a pint from the driver. Classic Peckham vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a toddler at the playground sharing snacks with a stray cat, I knew Neukölln felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a Döner and sit by the Spree, listening to a random street musician play a sax while a group of skaters busts out tricks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their nonchalance during BART delays. While newcomers panic, we just whip out snacks and settle in for the ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton this morning, and a guy in a full tuxedo hopped on with a pet iguana under his arm. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all Gaudí and tapas, but the real heart of Gràcia is the quirky plazas where locals swap books and gossip over homemade empanadas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still check the metro map like it’s a treasure map, but the locals just know which line to jump on without a glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' with a capital T when you mean the Overground, and you're baffled by the 41 bus going the wrong way.
Saw a guy on the 3 bus last night trying to sell a goldfish in a plastic bag. Shouted ‘Brixton’s finest!’ as he got off at Coldharbour Lane.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a stray cat guarding a kebab shop during a midnight rain. Felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can stumble upon impromptu street performances in the U-Bahn stations, like a saxophonist playing under the lights of Görlitzer Bahnhof—gives the morning commute a unexpected charm.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they shrug off the random BART delays, like it's part of their daily cardio routine. Tourists gasp, locals just sigh.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy in a tuxedo chasing after a Muni bus that just left. Only in SF would you dress up for a gala and still miss your ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Gràcia plazas are for lounging. They don't see locals using them as a stage for impromptu concerts and family gatherings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewildered faces staring at the metro map, still clutching a paper map instead of using their phones like everyone else.
